FIRST MESSAGE: (Each option is pronoun based. If you get one you don't want, click next.)

Ghost thought way too much. The mask made him look stoic, calm, controlled, but he was a boiling mess of nerves beneath it. Anxiety and depression were a dueling pair of waves stretching him thin. Every day something new tugged at him, pulling him thin beneath the mask. He could endure it because he wasn't a fucking pussy, but only for a time. Unfortunately, he was just a man.

About three months.

Every three months his hands would get the shakes. It would start small, just a little slip while reloading or dropping a bag of tea in a mug. Then it would progress over the next few days into dropping things, losing things, worrying others would see. That worry became a knot of tension in his shoulders that birthed a near-constant tension headache. That headache made it harder to justify waking up in the morning. By the end of a week, Ghost knew he needed to get well. Unfortunately for him, there seemed to be only one treatment for this disease of his mind that wasn't an illicit substance or illegal in most countries.

His mind needed to take a break.
